articles.headline: National Urban Resilience Mission Launched: India Aims for Climate-Proof Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has officially launched the National Urban Resilience Mission (NURM).
• The mission aims to integrate climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction into urban planning and infrastructure development.
• Key focus areas include developing resilient infrastructure, improving early warning systems, and enhancing urban green spaces.
• Funding will be provided through a combination of central schemes, state contributions, and international climate finance.
• NURM emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving local bodies, private sector, and community participation.

Examine the role of local self-governments and community participation in ensuring the success and sustainability of urban resilience initiatives.

articles.trivia: India is projected to have 600 million people living in urban areas by 2030, making urban resilience critical.

articles.headline: India-EU FTA Negotiations Enter Final Phase, Focus on Services and Green Tech
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have entered their conclusive phase, with both sides optimistic about an early conclusion.
• Key areas of discussion include market access for goods, services trade, investment protection, and intellectual property rights.
• A significant focus has been placed on 'green technologies' and sustainable trade practices, reflecting global environmental concerns.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for Indian businesses in the EU market.
• Challenges remain on issues such as tariffs on sensitive products and data localization norms.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Hypersonic Scramjet Engine for Future Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of an advanced hypersonic scramjet engine.
• This test marks a significant milestone in India's pursuit of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology and future space transportation systems.
• Scramjet engines allow for air-breathing propulsion, significantly reducing the amount of oxidizer needed onboard, thus increasing payload capacity and reducing launch costs.
• The technology is crucial for developing two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) and single-stage-to-orbit (SSTO) vehicles.
• This development places India among a select few nations possessing such advanced propulsion capabilities.

articles.headline: Inter-State Water Dispute Tribunal Recommends New Framework for River Basin Management
articles.explainer:
• An ad-hoc Inter-State Water Dispute Tribunal, constituted to resolve a long-standing river dispute, has submitted its recommendations to the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
• The tribunal has proposed a new framework focusing on integrated river basin management rather than just water allocation.
• Recommendations include establishing a permanent national river basin authority and promoting data sharing among riparian states.
• The report emphasizes climate change impacts on water availability and the need for adaptive management strategies.
• This development could set a precedent for resolving other complex inter-state water disputes in India.

Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure

articles.headline: RBI Proposes Stricter Norms for Digital Lending Platforms to Protect Consumers
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a new set of stricter regulations for digital lending platforms.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over predatory lending practices, exorbitant interest rates, and unethical recovery methods by some unregulated apps.
• Proposed norms include mandatory disclosures of all charges, a standardized format for Key Fact Statements, and restrictions on data collection by apps.
• The RBI aims to create a robust regulatory framework that fosters innovation while safeguarding consumer interests and financial stability.
• Public feedback is invited on the discussion paper before final guidelines are issued.

Discuss the role of technology and innovation in financial inclusion, and how regulation can strike a balance between fostering innovation and ensuring consumer safety.

articles.headline: India Unveils Comprehensive National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 Amid Rising Global Threats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0, updating the previous framework.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure, promoting cyber hygiene, and fostering international cooperation.
• Key pillars include robust threat intelligence, capacity building, R&D in emerging technologies, and a framework for public-private partnerships.
• It addresses the evolving landscape of cyber warfare, ransomware attacks, and data breaches, emphasizing proactive defense mechanisms.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for all citizens and businesses, vital for India's digital economy.

articles.headline: Expansion of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to Remote Areas Faces Implementation Challenges
articles.explainer:
• While the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has seen significant progress in urban and semi-urban areas, its expansion into remote and rural regions is encountering hurdles.
• Key challenges include lack of digital literacy, inadequate internet connectivity, limited availability of digital health infrastructure, and resistance to adoption among healthcare providers.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for health records, making healthcare accessible and efficient across the country.
• Government is exploring innovative solutions like mobile health units and community health workers to bridge the digital divide.
• Success in remote areas is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and truly transforming India's healthcare landscape.

articles.headline: Quad Nations Conduct Joint Naval Drills in Indo-Pacific, Emphasize Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The Quad group (India, US, Japan, Australia) concluded a series of joint naval exercises in the Indo-Pacific region, reaffirming their commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• The drills focused on interoperability, anti-submarine warfare, maritime surveillance, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• These exercises are seen as a strategic response to growing geopolitical complexities and assertiveness in the region.
• The Quad nations emphasized the importance of upholding international law, particularly the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS).
• While not explicitly an alliance, the Quad continues to strengthen coordination on security, economic, and technological fronts.

articles.headline: New Study Highlights Impact of Microplastics on Agricultural Soil Health in India
articles.explainer:
• A recent study published in the 'Environmental Science & Technology' journal has revealed widespread microplastic contamination in agricultural soils across various Indian states.
• The research indicates that microplastics, primarily from plastic mulching, sewage sludge, and industrial waste, are altering soil structure, water retention, and microbial activity.
• These changes can negatively impact crop yield, nutrient cycling, and potentially lead to the uptake of microplastics by plants, entering the food chain.
• The study calls for urgent policy interventions, including better waste management, promotion of biodegradable alternatives, and research into remediation technologies.
• This highlights a growing environmental concern with direct implications for food security and human health in India.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

Calls for better waste management and biodegradable alternatives.

Analyze the existing policies and practices related to plastic waste management in India and suggest comprehensive measures to mitigate microplastic pollution in agricultural ecosystems.
Evaluate the role of scientific research and technological innovation in developing sustainable solutions for microplastic remediation and prevention.
articles.imageDescription: Microplastic particles mixed with soil and plant roots, illustrating contamination.
articles.trivia: Microplastics are plastic pieces smaller than 5 millimeters, posing a significant threat to ecosystems.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Juvenile Justice Act for Enhanced Child Protection
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Social Justice and Empowerment has submitted a report recommending significant amendments to the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015.
• The recommendations aim to strengthen the child protection framework, streamline adoption processes, and enhance the rehabilitation of children in conflict with law.
• Key proposals include stricter oversight of child care institutions, faster disposal of adoption cases, and a greater emphasis on restorative justice for juveniles.
• The committee also addressed concerns regarding the definition of 'child' and the handling of serious offenses committed by minors.
• These reforms are crucial for aligning India's child protection laws with international standards and ensuring the best interests of children.

articles.headline: Government Considers Public-Private Partnership Model for Revitalizing Public Sector Banks
articles.explainer:
• The Union Finance Ministry is reportedly exploring a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model to infuse capital and improve efficiency in select Public Sector Banks (PSBs).
• This move aims to address the challenges of capital adequacy, non-performing assets (NPAs), and governance issues faced by some state-owned banks.
• The proposed model could involve private equity participation, management expertise sharing, and technology integration while maintaining government majority stake.
• The initiative seeks to make PSBs more competitive, customer-centric, and capable of supporting India's economic growth trajectory.
• Discussions are ongoing with stakeholders, including RBI and PSB management, to finalize the framework.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases Announced
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in the development of an indigenous vaccine targeting several Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
• The vaccine candidate, developed through collaboration between ICMR and a private pharmaceutical firm, has shown promising results in early-stage clinical trials.
• NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations in tropical and subtropical regions, including many parts of India, causing significant morbidity and mortality.
• This development is crucial for India's public health goals and contributes to global efforts to combat these often-overlooked diseases.
• Further trials and regulatory approvals are underway, with hopes for accelerated deployment in affected communities.

articles.headline: Community-Led Forest Management Initiatives Show Success in Eastern Ghats
articles.explainer:
• New reports highlight the success of community-led forest management programs in parts of the Eastern Ghats, particularly in Odisha and Andhra Pradesh.
• These initiatives, often involving tribal communities, focus on sustainable harvesting of minor forest produce, afforestation, and protection against illegal logging.
• The programs have led to significant improvements in forest cover, biodiversity conservation, and enhanced livelihoods for local communities.
• They demonstrate the effectiveness of involving local communities in conservation efforts, aligning with the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
• This model offers valuable lessons for replicating similar approaches in other ecologically sensitive regions of India.

articles.headline: India Pledges Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UNGA Session
articles.explainer:
• During the ongoing United Nations General Assembly (UNGA) session, India reiterated its commitment to climate action and pledged significant financial support for a new 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund'.
• The fund aims to assist developing nations, particularly Small Island Developing States (SIDS) and Least Developed Countries (LDCs), in building resilience against climate change impacts.
• India emphasized the principle of Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) in climate finance.
• This move solidifies India's leadership role among developing nations and its stance on climate justice.
• The fund will focus on projects related to early warning systems, climate-resilient agriculture, and sustainable infrastructure.

articles.headline: E-Governance Portal Launched to Streamline Land Record Management Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development has launched a new centralized e-governance portal for land record management, aiming to modernize and streamline the process nationwide.
• The portal integrates existing state-level digital land record systems, providing a unified platform for citizens to access land information, mutation, and registration services.
• This initiative is part of the Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP), focused on reducing land disputes, preventing fraudulent transactions, and enhancing transparency.
• Key features include geo-referencing of cadastral maps, integration with revenue courts, and provisions for online grievance redressal.
• While implementation across all states will take time, the portal promises to bring significant efficiency and accountability to land administration.

articles.headline: IMD Report Warns of Intensified Climate Change Impact on Indian Monsoon Patterns
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has released its annual report, detailing intensified and erratic monsoon patterns attributed to climate change.
• The report highlights an increase in extreme rainfall events, prolonged dry spells, and shifts in monsoon onset and withdrawal dates across various regions.
• These changes have significant implications for agriculture, water resources, and disaster management, leading to economic losses and humanitarian challenges.
• IMD emphasizes the need for enhanced climate modeling, improved early warning systems, and adaptive agricultural practices to mitigate future impacts.
• The findings underscore the urgency of global and national climate action, including emission reduction and adaptation strategies.

articles.headline: New Education Policy: Focus on Integrating Digital Learning and Vocational Skills
articles.explainer:

articles.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amid Hydrological Changes
articles.explainer:
• India has formally called for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing significant hydrological changes in the region.
• The Ministry of External Affairs stated that climate change impacts, including glacier melt and altered river flows, necessitate a re-evaluation of water sharing mechanisms.
• India emphasized that the review aims to ensure equitable and sustainable utilization of shared water resources, not to abrogate the treaty.
• Pakistan has expressed concerns, while international mediators are closely monitoring the situation, given the treaty's historical significance.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River system between India and Pakistan.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Urban Resilience Mission (NURM):

prelimsMocks.question:
Scramjet engines, recently tested by ISRO, are crucial for future space missions primarily because they:
(a) Use a liquid propellant mixture to achieve higher thrust in vacuum.
(b) Allow for air-breathing propulsion, reducing onboard oxidizer requirements.
(c) Are designed for deep-space exploration beyond Earth's orbit.
(d) Utilize nuclear fusion for propulsion, enabling faster interplanetary travel.
options: (a) Use a liquid propellant mixture to achieve higher thrust in vacuum.
(b) Allow for air-breathing propulsion, reducing onboard oxidizer requirements.
(c) Are designed for deep-space exploration beyond Earth's orbit.
(d) Utilize nuclear fusion for propulsion, enabling faster interplanetary travel.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Allow for air-breathing propulsion, reducing onboard oxidizer requirements.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Scramjet engines are a type of jet engine that use air from the atmosphere for combustion, meaning they do not need to carry a heavy oxidizer tank like traditional rockets. This significantly reduces the weight and cost of launch vehicles, making them crucial for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.

GS1.headline: IMD Report Warns of Intensified Climate Change Impact on Indian Monsoon Patterns
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has released its annual report, detailing intensified and erratic monsoon patterns attributed to climate change.
• The report highlights an increase in extreme rainfall events, prolonged dry spells, and shifts in monsoon onset and withdrawal dates across various regions.
• These changes have significant implications for agriculture, water resources, and disaster management, leading to economic losses and humanitarian challenges.
• IMD emphasizes the need for enhanced climate modeling, improved early warning systems, and adaptive agricultural practices to mitigate future impacts.
• The findings underscore the urgency of global and national climate action, including emission reduction and adaptation strategies.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Juvenile Justice Act for Enhanced Child Protection

• These reforms are crucial for aligning India's child protection laws with international standards and ensuring the best interests of children.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Hypersonic Scramjet Engine for Future Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground test of an advanced hypersonic scramjet engine.
• This test marks a significant milestone in India's pursuit of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology and future space transportation systems.
• Scramjet engines allow for air-breathing propulsion, significantly reducing the amount of oxidizer needed onboard, thus increasing payload capacity and reducing launch costs.
• The technology is crucial for developing two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) and single-stage-to-orbit (SSTO) vehicles.
• This development places India among a select few nations possessing such advanced propulsion capabilities.

GS3.headline: RBI Proposes Stricter Norms for Digital Lending Platforms to Protect Consumers
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a new set of stricter regulations for digital lending platforms.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over predatory lending practices, exorbitant interest rates, and unethical recovery methods by some unregulated apps.
• Proposed norms include mandatory disclosures of all charges, a standardized format for Key Fact Statements, and restrictions on data collection by apps.
• The RBI aims to create a robust regulatory framework that fosters innovation while safeguarding consumer interests and financial stability.
• Public feedback is invited on the discussion paper before final guidelines are issued.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Comprehensive National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0 Amid Rising Global Threats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2.0, updating the previous framework.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure, promoting cyber hygiene, and fostering international cooperation.
• Key pillars include robust threat intelligence, capacity building, R&D in emerging technologies, and a framework for public-private partnerships.
• It addresses the evolving landscape of cyber warfare, ransomware attacks, and data breaches, emphasizing proactive defense mechanisms.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for all citizens and businesses, vital for India's digital economy.

GS3.headline: New Study Highlights Impact of Microplastics on Agricultural Soil Health in India
GS3.explainer:
• A recent study published in the 'Environmental Science & Technology' journal has revealed widespread microplastic contamination in agricultural soils across various Indian states.
• The research indicates that microplastics, primarily from plastic mulching, sewage sludge, and industrial waste, are altering soil structure, water retention, and microbial activity.
• These changes can negatively impact crop yield, nutrient cycling, and potentially lead to the uptake of microplastics by plants, entering the food chain.
• The study calls for urgent policy interventions, including better waste management, promotion of biodegradable alternatives, and research into remediation technologies.
• This highlights a growing environmental concern with direct implications for food security and human health in India.

GS3.headline: Government Considers Public-Private Partnership Model for Revitalizing Public Sector Banks
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Finance Ministry is reportedly exploring a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) model to infuse capital and improve efficiency in select Public Sector Banks (PSBs).
• This move aims to address the challenges of capital adequacy, non-performing assets (NPAs), and governance issues faced by some state-owned banks.
• The proposed model could involve private equity participation, management expertise sharing, and technology integration while maintaining government majority stake.
• The initiative seeks to make PSBs more competitive, customer-centric, and capable of supporting India's economic growth trajectory.
• Discussions are ongoing with stakeholders, including RBI and PSB management, to finalize the framework.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Vaccine Development for Neglected Tropical Diseases Announced
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists have announced a significant breakthrough in the development of an indigenous vaccine targeting several Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs).
• The vaccine candidate, developed through collaboration between ICMR and a private pharmaceutical firm, has shown promising results in early-stage clinical trials.
• NTDs disproportionately affect vulnerable populations in tropical and subtropical regions, including many parts of India, causing significant morbidity and mortality.
• This development is crucial for India's public health goals and contributes to global efforts to combat these often-overlooked diseases.
• Further trials and regulatory approvals are underway, with hopes for accelerated deployment in affected communities.

GS3.headline: Community-Led Forest Management Initiatives Show Success in Eastern Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• New reports highlight the success of community-led forest management programs in parts of the Eastern Ghats, particularly in Odisha and Andhra Pradesh.
• These initiatives, often involving tribal communities, focus on sustainable harvesting of minor forest produce, afforestation, and protection against illegal logging.
• The programs have led to significant improvements in forest cover, biodiversity conservation, and enhanced livelihoods for local communities.
• They demonstrate the effectiveness of involving local communities in conservation efforts, aligning with the Forest Rights Act (FRA), 2006.
• This model offers valuable lessons for replicating similar approaches in other ecologically sensitive regions of India.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Sees Record Applications from Tier-2 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has reported a significant surge in applications from startups based in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities across India.
• This indicates a growing entrepreneurial ecosystem beyond major metropolitan centers, driven by increased digital penetration and government support.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) highlights the success in fostering innovation and creating employment opportunities in smaller cities.
• This trend is crucial for balanced regional development and achieving the vision of an 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India).

GS3.headline: India Participates in Multi-National Anti-Terror Exercise 'Dharma Guardian' with Japan
GS3.explainer:
• Indian Army contingent participated in the joint military exercise 'Dharma Guardian' with the Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF) in a multi-national format.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability in counter-terrorism operations, special forces tactics, and intelligence sharing in urban and semi-urban terrains.
• This year's iteration included observers from other friendly nations, signaling broader security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region.
• 'Dharma Guardian' aims to foster strategic partnership and share best practices between the two forces.
• The exercise underscores the growing defense cooperation between India and Japan, crucial for regional stability.
